content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 30, 2011


Theresa M. Garcia
3048 Corona Street
Lemon Grove, CA 91945-2713

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-11-199

Dear Ms. Garcia:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You were the treasurer for the Committee to Elect B. Toby Mange in connection with the November 11, 2002 election. You are a member of Mr. Mange's family. The campaign was unsuccessful and after the election the Committee disbanded. It was your first time as a treasurer. The bank account for the Committee was closed soon after the election. You did not realize that a final statement (Form 410) was required to be filed to terminate the Committee. In January of 2007, Mr. Mange passed away in Mesa, Arizona.

You received a warning letter from the FPPC dated January 31, 2011, which informed you of the Committee's violations for failure to file semi-annual campaign statements since just before the election in 2002 to present.

Since you received the warning letter, you have been trying to rectify the situation. However, it has been over seven years since the bank account was used, the bank does not have any records available. You no longer have any records for this account or for the Committee.

You would like to know what your obligations are with respect filing statements for the Committee.

We advise you to file a Form 410 (Statement of Organization) with the Secretary of State indicating your intent to terminate this committee and include a letter with the form describing

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-11-199
Page No. 2


the circumstances surrounding your inability to obtain records for this committee. Also include
in the letter that you have received a warning letter from the FPPC with respect to the violations
of the Committee.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By:    Sukhi K. Brar
       Counsel, Legal Division

SKB:jgl

sections.conclusion: We advise you to file a Form 410 (Statement of Organization) with the Secretary of State indicating your intent to terminate this committee and include a letter with the form describing the circumstances surrounding your inability to obtain records for this committee. Also include in the letter that you have received a warning letter from the FPPC with respect to the violations of the Committee.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the obligations of a former committee treasurer regarding filing statements for a campaign committee that disbanded in 2002 but never filed a final termination statement, particularly when records are no longer available?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises the requester to file a Form 410 termination statement with the Secretary of State along with an explanatory letter describing the circumstances of the missing records and the prior FPPC warning letter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question context and conclusion. No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but question and conclusion are clearly identifiable from document structure. Document discusses past violations and provides clear directive for compliance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the obligations of a former committee treasurer regarding filing statements for a campaign committee that disbanded in 2002 but never filed a final termination statement, particularly when records are no longer available?

CONCLUSION: We advise you to file a Form 410 (Statement of Organization) with the Secretary of State indicating your intent to terminate this committee and include a letter with the form describing the circumstances surrounding your inability to obtain records for this committee. Also include in the letter that you have received a warning letter from the FPPC with respect to the violations of the Committee.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Theresa M. Garcia regarding her obligations as treasurer of a 2002 campaign committee that never filed a final termination statement. The FPPC advises her to file a Form 410 with the Secretary of State along with an explanatory letter detailing the circumstances and prior violations.
